title: नरसिंह पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

त्वत्प्रभुजीवप्रियमिच्छसि चेन्नरहरिपूजां कुरु सततम्।
प्रतिबिम्बालङ्कृतिधृतिकुशलो बिम्बालङ्कृतिमुद्वहति॥
चेतोभृङ्ग भ्रमसि वृथा भवमरुभूमौ विरसायाम्।
भज भज लक्ष्मीनरसिंहानघपदसरसिजमकरन्दम्॥ १॥

शठकोपमुनिवचनशतकोटिव्याख्यानघनसारम्।
भवभयवारणदक्षं शरणागतवज्रपञ्जरमनन्तम्॥
कालत्रयचापलमनलम् पापक्षयकारणं शुभदम्।
भज भज लक्ष्मीनरसिंहानघपदसरसिजमकरन्दम्॥ २॥

शोषणभीषणरोषैरुग्रस्तम्भोद्भवं हरिवरिष्ठम्।
हिरण्यकशिपुच्छेदनविदारणवर्णनप्रवणमनिशम्॥
प्रह्लादवरदमनघं पदमभयदं श्रीयुतं भजत सकलम्।
भज भज लक्ष्मीनरसिंहानघपदसरसिजमकरन्दम्॥ ३॥

ज्वालाजटालमत्युग्रमुत्फुल्लनयनत्रयं हरिम्।
कराळवदनं स्फुरत् सटाशतकेसरीमुखम्॥
वराहमत्स्यकूर्मादिदशाकृतिमनघं प्रभुम्।
भज भज लक्ष्मीनरसिंहानघपदसरसिजमकरन्दम्॥ ४॥

माणिक्यमौलिविलसत् स्फुरद्दिव्यकिरीटधृक्।
सहस्रफणिभूषाङ्गो ज्वलत्कुण्डलमण्डितः॥
सर्वभूतहिते रतो नित्यं कल्याणदायकः।
भज भज लक्ष्मीनरसिंहानघपदसरसिजमकरन्दम्॥ ५॥

॥ इति श्री नरसिंह पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Pancharatnam ||

Tvatprabhujivapryam Ichchhasi Chen Naraharipujam Kuru Satatam |
Pratibimbalankritidhriti Kushalo Bimbalankritim Udvahati ||
Chetobhringa Bhramasi Vritha Bhavamarubhumau Virasayam |
Bhaja Bhaja Lakshminarasimhanaghapadasarasijam Akarandam || 1 ||

Shathakopamunivachanashatakoti Vyakhyanaghanasaram |
Bhavabhayavaranadaksham Sharanagata Vajrapanjaram Anantam ||
Kalatrayachapalam Analam Papakshayakaranam Shubhadam |
Bhaja Bhaja Lakshminarasimhanaghapadasarasijam Akarandam || 2 ||

Shoshanabhishanaroshai Rugrastambhodbhavam Harivaristham |
Hiranyakashipu Chhedana Vidarana Varnana Pravanam Anisham ||
Prahladavaradamanagham Padamabhayadam Shriyutam Bhajata Sakalam |
Bhaja Bhaja Lakshminarasimhanaghapadasarasijam Akarandam || 3 ||

Jvalajatalam Atyugram Utphullanayanatrayam Harim |
Karalavadanam Sphurat Satashatakesarimukham ||
Varahamatsyakurmadi Dashakritim Anagham Prabhum |
Bhaja Bhaja Lakshminarasimhanaghapadasarasijam Akarandam || 4 ||

Manikyamaulivilasat Sphuraddivyakiritadhrik |
Sahasraphanibhushango Jvalat Kundala Manditah ||
Sarvabhutahite Rato Nityam Kalyanadayakah |
Bhaja Bhaja Lakshminarasimhanaghapadasarasijam Akarandam || 5 ||

|| Iti Shri Narasimha Pancharatnam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
यदि तुम अपने प्रभु जीव का प्रिय चाहते हो तो सदा नरहरि की पूजा करो।

हे चित्तरूपी भ्रमर, संसार के मरुभूमि में व्यर्थ भटकना छोड़ो और लक्ष्मीनरसिंह के निष्पाप चरणकमल का मकरन्द सेवन करो।

हिरण्यकशिपु को विदीर्ण करने वाले, प्रह्लाद को वरदान देने वाले, स्तम्भ से प्रकट उग्ररूपधारी नरसिंह भज।

ज्वलंत जटा, उग्र मुख, सहस्र फणों के भूषण वाले, सर्वभूतहित में रत श्री नरसिंह की स्तुति करो।
benefits: Five jewel-verses of Lord Narasimha by Adi Shankaracharya
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fear and grants divine protection
Traditionally recited for removal of sins and negative karma across three time periods
Invokes the fierce protective form of Vishnu
Ideal for Narasimha Jayanti and daily protection prayers
description: Narasimha Pancharatnam (नरसिंह पञ्चरत्नम्) is five gems of devotional verses dedicated to Lord Narasimha. Attributed to Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to invoke the fierce protective form of Vishnu, remove all fear and grant divine protection, and destroy sins and negative karma across three time periods. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 5 verses and takes around 4 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Pancharatnam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five jewel-verses of Lord Narasimha for divine protection and fear removal.

Bhaja bhaja Lakshmi Narasimha pada saroja makaranda
Narasimha Pancharatnam audio MP3 chant download
Narasimha Pancharatnam for Narasimha Jayanti Vaishakha
Cheto bhringa bhramasi vritha bhava maru bhumau
Narasimha Pancharatnam for Swati nakshatra Chaturdashi

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Pancharatnam (नरसिंह पञ्चरत्नम्) is a five-verse (pancharatnam — five jewels) Sanskrit devotional hymn composed by Sri Adi Shankaracharya in praise of Bhagavan Lakshmi-Narasimha — the man-lion fourth avatara of Vishnu. Each of the five verses ends with the magnificent refrain Bhaja bhaja Lakshmi-Narasimha-anagha-pada-sarasija-makarandam — Drink, drink the nectar from the lotus of the sinless feet of Lakshmi-Narasimha — addressed to the wandering bee of the mind (cheto-bhringa). The verses describe the deity as worshipped through the Sri Vaishnava Shathakopa-muni traditions, the protector of refugees (sharanagata-vajra-panjara), the destroyer of Hiranyakashipu, the supreme refuge whom the mind should drink as a bee drinks nectar from a lotus, rather than wandering vainly in the parched desert (maru-bhumi) of samsara. (Source: Adi Shankara stotra corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous metaphor used in the Narasimha Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Pancharatnam uses one of the most beautiful and oft-quoted metaphors in the entire Adi Shankara corpus: the mind (cheto) is compared to a wandering bee (bhringa) that flies vainly (vritha bhramasi) in the parched, tasteless desert (virasayam maru-bhumi) of worldly existence (bhava) seeking honey it will never find — and is repeatedly urged to settle instead on the lotus feet of Lakshmi-Narasimha to drink the inexhaustible nectar (makaranda). The refrain Cheto-bhringa bhramasi vritha bhava-maru-bhumau virasayam, bhaja bhaja Lakshmi-Narasimha-anagha-pada-sarasija-makarandam transforms the entire stotra into a stern Advaitic instruction to the wandering mind to abandon worldly seeking and turn inward to bhakti at the divine feet. The image is one of Shankara's most powerful poetic creations. (Source: Adi Shankara Narasimha Pancharatnam.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Narasimha Pancharatnam and at which kshetra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Pancharatnam is traditionally attributed to Sri Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE). According to the Shankara Digvijaya tradition, Shankara composed his various Narasimha stotras during his pilgrimage to the great Narasimha kshetras — particularly Ahobilam in the Nallamala hills of Andhra Pradesh, the supreme Narasimha shrine where the Nava Narasimha (nine forms — Jvala, Ahobila, Malola, Kroda, Karancha, Bhargava, Yogananda, Chatravata, and Pavana Narasimha) are worshipped on the cliff caves where the avatara is said to have manifested. The Pancharatnam, the Karavalamba Stotram, and the Lakshmi Narasimha Pancharatnam form a trilogy of Shankara's Narasimha hymns. They are recited as the daily liturgy at Ahobilam, Yadagirigutta (Telangana), Mangalagiri, Simhachalam (Andhra Pradesh), and Sholingur (Tamil Nadu). (Sources: Madhaviya Shankara Vijayam; Ahobilam temple tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Narasimha Pancharatnam and the Lakshmi Narasimha Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are 5-verse Adi Shankara compositions on the same deity but are distinct hymns with different opening lines, refrains and emphases. The Lakshmi Narasimha Pancharatnam (Tvat-prabhu-jiva-priyam ichhasi chen narahari-pujam kuru satatam) emphasises the iconography — eight-armed (ashta-bhuja), elephant-strong (matta-gajendra), embraced by Lakshmi (Lakshmi-aalingita-vama-bhaga) — and ends each verse with Lakshmi-Narasimham aham prapadye (I take refuge in Lakshmi-Narasimha). The Narasimha Pancharatnam (Tvat-prabhu-jiva-priyam ichhasi chen ... cheto-bhringa bhramasi) shares the opening line in many recensions but builds toward the famous bee-and-lotus metaphor and ends each verse with Bhaja bhaja Lakshmi-Narasimha-anagha-pada-sarasija-makarandam. The two are sister-hymns; many devotees recite both together as a single integrated upasana. (Source: Adi Shankara Narasimha stotra corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Narasimha Pancharatnam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Pancharatnam is most powerful when chanted on Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi at twilight — the avatara's exact muhurta), every Swati nakshatra (Narasimha's birth-star), every Chaturdashi tithi (especially of the bright fortnight), throughout Vaishakha, on Saturdays (Narasimha protects from Shani's afflictions), at the Brahmotsavam of Ahobilam (Phalguna Shukla Ekadashi to Krishna Pratipada — the 11-day festival), Yadagirigutta and Mangalagiri, during periods of severe psychological distress, persecution, or fear, and as a daily practice for Sri Vaishnava and Smarta devotees of Narasimha. Initiated upasakas of the Narasimha mantra recite it as the daily prelude to their japa. The Pancharatnam combined with the Karavalamba Stotram is the standard Shankara-Narasimha liturgy. (Sources: Drikpanchang Narasimha calendar; Ahobilam temple tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narasimha Pancharatnam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Narasimha Pancharatnam: (1) bhava-bhaya-varana — protection from the great fear of samsara (the principal Advaitic goal embedded in the bee-and-lotus metaphor); (2) sharanagata-vajra-panjara — refuge in the diamond-cage of the deity who protects all surrendered devotees as he protected Prahlada; (3) papa-kshaya — destruction of accumulated sins (papa-kshaya-karanam, declared in verse 2); (4) shubha-da — bestowal of all auspiciousness; (5) protection from grahas, evil spirits, persecuting authorities, and unjust enemies (since Hiranyakashipu was the archetype of tyrannical authority); (6) victory in seemingly impossible situations (the avatara himself transcended every limiting condition); and (7) ultimately moksha — liberation through Lakshmi-Narasimha bhakti, the very purpose for which Shankara composed the stotra. (Source: Adi Shankara phalashruti; Narasimha bhakti tradition.)
